Off Calendar Feb-05-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF CHERIE POWER's Motion To Compel Defendant Ignacio Cong Fong's Further Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Set Two, Number 15.1 And Requests For Documents, Set One; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant Ignacio Cong Fong And His Attorney Of Record, Jimmie Williams, In The Amount Of $1,719.95

Form Interrogatory #15 is Granted. Defendants' preliminary statement is stricken as CCP sec. 2030.210 does not allow for either a Preliminary Statement or a General Objection. All objections are overruled. When responding to interrogatories, the Discovery Act requires a party to make a reasonable and good faith effort to obtain the information before responding to the interrogatories. Regency Health Services, Inc. v. Superior Court (1998) 64 CA4th 1496. A party cannot plead ignorance to information, which can be obtained from sources under his control. Deyo v. Kilbourne (1978) 84 CA3d 771, 782 This includes a party's lawyer Smith v. Superior Court (Alfred) (1961) 189 CA2d 6, agents or employees Gordon v. Sup. Ct. (1984) 161 CA 3d 151, 167-168, family members Jones v. Superior Court (1981) 119 CA 3d 534, 552. See Weil and Brown, Cal. Prac. Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (TRG 2018) 8:1051-1060. This means that an attorney can't just pawn off the responses to the client or spend an hour and dictate the responses off the top of his head. See Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants (2007) 148 CA4th 390. The court finds that defendants responses fail to meet his obligations under the Discovery Act. Document Requests #1, 2, are 10 are granted. Defendants' preliminary statement is stricken as CCP sec. 2031.210-240 does not allow for either a Preliminary Statement or a General Objection. All objections are overruled. Defendants' response is not in compliance with C.C.P. sec. 2031.230 which states: "A representation of inability to comply with the particular demand for inspection, copying, testing, or sampling shall affirm that a diligent search and a reasonable inquiry has been made in an effort to comply with that demand. This statement shall also specify whether the inability to comply is because the particular item or category has never existed, has been destroyed, has been lost, misplaced, or stolen, or has never been, or is no longer, in the possession, custody, or control of the responding party. The statement shall set forth the name and address of any natural person or organization known or believed by that party to have possession, custody, or control of that item or category of item." Defendants has also failed to provide a privilege log for the documents for documents they are withholding on a claim of privilege as required by C.C.P. sec. 2031.240. It is bad faith for defendants to claim a privilege for documents that do not exist. Bihun v. AT&T Info. Sys. (1993) 13 CA4th 976, 991. Verified Responses to Form Interrogatory 15.1 in compliance with CCP sec. 2030.210 to be served no later than February 26, 2021. Verified responses to Requests for Production of Documents in compliance with CCP sec. 2031.210-240 to be served no later than February 26, 2021. Documents to be produced in compliance with CCP sec. 2031.280 no later than February 26, 2021. Requests for sanctions is granted in the amount of $1444.95. Payment to be made to the Client Trust Fund of the Law Offices of Mary Catherine Widerhold no later than February 26, 2021.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312 via email toklgallo@discoveryreferee.com
